_doc: The neutral-noun → per-platform-concrete map. The core (PROTOCOL.md, the roles, the checklists) names every act with a neutral noun; this one table resolves each to its platform tool. A reader who needs the concrete consults this; the prose stays neutral. Single home — never copy this mapping elsewhere. Adding a platform = add a column.

class_support._doc: Which enforcement return classes each platform realises. A rule whose class is unsupported falls back to persona (recorded per-rule in deny-rules.json `fallback`).

models._doc: Per-platform model policy for the project config's role `model` field (ai-pm.config.json). ZERO-CONFIG by default — works with no setup. An allowlist (deny by default): a model is usable for a role only if in `allow`, so the list never lags the model space. `session` = the orchestrator's model. `auto` = a different in-allow model for independent blind spots IF the platform offers one out of the box, ELSE it falls back to `session` (never an error). A config pin must be in `allow`. Names are environment-dependent — that is why this lives in the adapter, not the neutral config. Cross-model beyond the built-in default is opt-in: extend `allow`.

claude.auto: the allow-listed model opposite the session's (opus<->sonnet) — always available, so cross-model review works zero-config

opencode._note: Empty allow by default ⇒ one session model for everything (zero-config; OpenCode has no guaranteed second review-grade model). To OPT IN to cross-model review, add approved provider/model ids to `allow` and set `auto` to one of them (it may be a different provider/class than the session).
